routes.title: Primary SPEC asset route
routes.summary: Clarify GitHub 666ghj/MiroFish, derive the durable SPEC tree, then expand documents, preview, and implementation prompts.

capabilities.purpose: Read repository context before route generation.

capabilities.purpose: Shape modules, dependencies, and engineering landing risks.

capabilities.purpose: Run repository inspection and artifact generation in isolation.

capabilities.purpose: Turn route nodes into SPEC tree candidates.

steps.title: Clarify execution intent
steps.description: Collect target users, product boundary, constraints, and success criteria before route choice.

steps.title: Scan GitHub source
steps.description: Inspect repositories and extract technology stack, module boundaries, and reusable assets.

steps.title: Map capability pool
steps.description: Choose Docker, MCP, skills, AIGC nodes, and specialist roles for analysis coverage.

steps.title: Derive SPEC tree seed
steps.description: Transform primary and alternative route nodes into an editable SPEC tree asset.

steps.title: Plan previews and prompts
steps.description: Prepare the downstream effect preview, architecture diagram, and implementation prompt package.

routes.title: Documentation-first conservative route
routes.summary: Create a narrower SPEC tree first, freeze requirements/design/tasks, then preview and package prompts after review.
routes.rationale: Reduces downstream churn when the business boundary is still broad or governance matters more than speed.

nodes.summary: Reserve the later execution bridge that turns accepted SPEC assets into repository changes and run evidence.

nodes.title: Preview-first exploratory route
nodes.summary: Push route analysis toward effect preview early, then backfill SPEC documents from the selected prototype direction.

nodes.title: Specification document generation
nodes.summary: Expand the selected SPEC tree into requirements, design, and tasks for each important node.

nodes.title: Effect preview
nodes.summary: Preview architecture, progress plan, expected UI/prototype direction, and step-by-step implementation effect before coding.

nodes.title: Implementation prompt package
nodes.summary: Package the selected future implementation into prompts that can be used by Cursor, Kiro, Trae, Windsurf, Codex, Claude, and similar tools.

nodes.title: Engineering landing
nodes.summary: Reserve the later execution bridge that turns accepted SPEC assets into repository changes and run evidence.

# Requirements: Documentation-first conservative route

## Summary

Create a narrower SPEC tree first, freeze requirements/design/tasks, then preview and package prompts after review.

## Inputs

- Node type: alternative_route
- Status: seed
- Priority: 6
- Dependencies: none
- Outputs: RouteSet outline, Decision evidence, SPEC tree seed, Architecture notes, Implementation prompt seed

## Derived Content

- The system shall support Create a narrower SPEC tree first, freeze requirements/design/tasks, then preview and package prompts after review..
- The node "Documentation-first conservative route" shall remain traceable through job artifacts.
- Downstream outputs: RouteSet outline, Decision evidence, SPEC tree seed, Architecture notes, Implementation prompt seed.
